subject: Re: Barretta Closing
body:
when, does har adom close

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> Gideon told me that Barretta's sale will close on
> Thursday.  The  proceeds of 
> that sale $13,935,660, will be used to purchase the
> replacement  property 
> from the accommodator who borrowed money from you to
> make the  replacement 
> property purchase.  The accommodator is going to
> wire the funds  it receives for the 
> replacement property back to you.  Rich Kahn asked
> me  if there was any 
> reason that you might want the money you receive to
> be  transferred back to FTC.  
> If so, he wondered whether or not we should wire 
> directly to FTC.
>  
> I remind you that the loan to the accommodator is in
> your name individually  
> and not in the name of FTC.  We probably don't want
> a paper record which  
> could lead someone mistakenly to believe that FTC,
> rather than you, entered  into 
> a real estate transaction in the states. 
> Consequently, even if you  decide 
> that you will wire the funds back to FTC, it is
> probably cleaner and more  
> appropriate that the accommodator wire the funds to
> you, and not FTC.   Please 
> advise how you wish to handle the matter.
>

body_html:
when, does har adom close

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Gideon told me that Barretta's sale will close on
Thursday. The proceeds of
that sale $13,935,660, will be used to purchase the
replacement property
from the accommodator who borrowed money from you to
make the replacement
property purchase. The accommodator is going to
wire the funds it receives for the
replacement property back to you. Rich Kahn asked
me if there was any
reason that you might want the money you receive to
be transferred back to FTC.
If so, he wondered whether or not we should wire
directly to FTC.

I remind you that the loan to the accommodator is in
your name individually
and not in the name of FTC. We probably don't want
a paper record which
could lead someone mistakenly to believe that FTC,
rather than you, entered into
a real estate transaction in the states.
Consequently, even if you decide
that you will wire the funds back to FTC, it is
probably cleaner and more
appropriate that the accommodator wire the funds to
you, and not FTC. Please
advise how you wish to handle the matter.

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
